title: exocarpos psilotiformis
intro: Exocarpos psilotiformis, also known as the woolly-barked ironwood, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its unique, woolly bark and small, yellowish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Exocarpos psilotiformis include the potential treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and fever, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or as a topical ointment, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential side effects.
